blocking_issues: The cover letter does not explain a credible candidate-specific motivation for Medica Europe's role, European portfolio, cross-functional scope, Lyon location or hybrid context; it states fit and potential contribution without saying why the work genuinely interests the candidate.
The cover letter lacks a sourced reason for targeting this particular role, domain scope or company beyond naming Medica Europe and its European medical-device portfolio.
warnings: Continuous improvement of regulatory processes is only indirectly evidenced through submission readiness, response closure and traceable follow-up work.
Priority management is inferable from concurrent responsibilities but is not stated explicitly.

The letter does not mention maintaining current submission plans, Product Development, Manufacturing, ISO 13485, CAPA or document control; these points are substantially covered elsewhere or are non-central to the letter's selected argument.
The letter page is visually sparse, but this is not independently blocking; the required repair is substantive motivation and target specificity, not filler or page expansion.

target_specificity.rationale: The letter names Medica Europe and the European medical-device portfolio, but it does not give a sourced reason why this particular portfolio, cross-functional setting, role scope or company is being targeted.

evidence_to_missions.rationale: The letter selects verified EU MDR documentation, change assessment, traceable coordination and post-market evidence, then connects those examples directly to submissions and lifecycle regulatory support.
evidence_to_missions.supporting_excerpt: This experience would enable me to support Medica Europe’s submissions while keeping documentation and cross-functional contributions aligned and auditable.

candidate_contribution.rationale: The candidate explicitly states how her experience would contribute to auditable submissions and portfolio-wide regulatory support across the device lifecycle.
candidate_contribution.supporting_excerpt: This combination of pre-market documentation and post-market compliance would allow me to support the regulatory needs of your European medical-device portfolio throughout the device lifecycle.

motivation_credibility.rationale: No sentence explains why a sourced feature of the role or context genuinely interests the candidate. Applying, matching experience and stating what she could support do not establish motivation.

tone_and_naturalness.rationale: The letter is concise, professional and coherent, with a natural progression from central documentation evidence to cross-functional coordination and lifecycle compliance.
tone_and_naturalness.supporting_excerpt: In my current regulatory affairs work, I coordinate traceable evidence across quality, clinical and engineering teams and follow notified-body questions through closure.

requirement_coverage.rationale: The package explicitly states three years of medical-device compliance experience and shows regulatory affairs and quality-assurance roles from 2022 to 2026.
EU MDR technical-documentation preparation and maintenance are stated explicitly and supported by the Class II device experience.
Change-impact assessments are explicit, although the rendered documents do not separately distinguish product changes from manufacturing changes.
Regulatory-submission evidence, traceability and notified-body question closure are explicit; keeping submission plans current is not stated.
Clinical evaluation, post-market surveillance, vigilance and risk-management documentation are all explicitly covered.
Submission-readiness reviews, question closure and traceable quality follow-up demonstrate relevant process discipline, but continuous improvement is not explicitly claimed.
EU MDR and ISO 13485 are both explicit and tied to relevant documentation and quality work.

Technical writing is explicit, English is recorded at C1 and the regulatory-documentation experience supports clear technical writing, though the experience bullet does not explicitly say the documents were written in English.
Concurrent documentation, submissions, notified-body and post-market duties imply priority management, but the behaviour is not stated directly.
The package explicitly describes traceable cross-functional coordination across quality, clinical and engineering contributors.
Class II devices, CAPA, document control and notified-body interactions are all explicitly present and grounded in experience.
English C1 directly establishes professional English proficiency, and the entire package is written consistently in English.
Confident cross-functional communication is demonstrated through coordination of quality, clinical and engineering teams and notified-body responses.

repair_actions.instruction: Add one concise, evidence-based sentence explaining why Sofia is genuinely interested in this role's sourced context, such as supporting a European medical-device portfolio across pre-market and post-market work in a cross-functional environment. Connect that interest to her verified lifecycle compliance experience without inventing company knowledge or personal history.

warnings: Continuous improvement of regulatory processes is supported only indirectly through controlled-document, CAPA, audit-follow-up and lifecycle work; no unsupported ownership claim was added.
Priority management and maintenance of submission plans are not stated explicitly, although the rendered experience demonstrates concurrent regulatory activities and notified-body follow-up.
Change-impact assessment is well evidenced, but the rendered documents do not specifically distinguish product changes from manufacturing changes.

target_specificity.rationale: The opening identifies the sourced European portfolio, EU MDR documentation, lifecycle-compliance and cross-functional scope as the specific reasons for applying.
target_specificity.supporting_excerpt: I am applying for the Regulatory Affairs Specialist position because its focus on a European medical-device portfolio combines the work I have built my experience around: rigorous EU MDR documentation, lifecycle compliance and coordination across specialist teams.

evidence_to_missions.rationale: Verified documentation, change-assessment, traceability and notified-body evidence is directly connected to supporting Medica Europe’s regulatory submissions.
evidence_to_missions.supporting_excerpt: Over three years in medical-device compliance, I have prepared and maintained EU MDR technical documentation for Class II devices, including change-impact assessments. I have also coordinated traceable evidence across quality, clinical and engineering teams and followed notified-body questions through closure.

candidate_contribution.rationale: The letter explicitly states the practical contribution the candidate would make to submissions and ongoing portfolio compliance.
candidate_contribution.supporting_excerpt: This experience would allow me to support Medica Europe’s submissions with clear documentation, disciplined traceability and effective cross-functional follow-up.

motivation_credibility.rationale: The candidate links genuine interest in the role’s sourced full-lifecycle scope to her verified post-market, clinical, vigilance and risk-management experience.
motivation_credibility.supporting_excerpt: The role’s full-lifecycle scope is particularly compelling to me. My work has included clinical-evaluation and risk-management updates, post-market surveillance and vigilance activities, giving me a practical understanding of how submission evidence must remain coherent as devices and safety information evolve.

tone_and_naturalness.rationale: The writing is concise, professional and cohesive, with specific evidence and no conspicuous boilerplate, repetition or keyword stuffing.
tone_and_naturalness.supporting_excerpt: I would bring that perspective to maintaining the compliance of Medica Europe’s portfolio beyond initial approval.

requirement_coverage.rationale: The CV and letter explicitly state three years of medical-device compliance experience across regulatory affairs and quality assurance.
Both documents explicitly state preparation and maintenance of EU MDR technical documentation.
Change-impact assessment is explicit, but product and manufacturing changes are not separately named.
Submission evidence, traceability and notified-body question closure are explicit; maintaining submission plans is not separately stated.
Clinical evaluation, post-market surveillance, vigilance and risk-management documentation are all explicitly covered.
Lifecycle updates, CAPA evidence, controlled documents and traceable audit follow-up support improvement work without claiming direct process-improvement ownership.
EU MDR and ISO 13485 are both explicitly evidenced through experience, skills and training.
Technical-document production and technical writing are explicit, English C1 is visible, and the rendered English letter demonstrates clear professional writing.
Concurrent documentation, submission, notified-body, lifecycle and audit-follow-up activities imply priority management, but the behaviour is not explicitly claimed.
Cross-functional coordination across quality, clinical and engineering teams is explicitly tied to traceable evidence and closure tracking.
Class II devices, CAPA, document control and notified-body interactions are all explicitly present and supported.
English C1 is explicit, while confident cross-functional communication is demonstrated through coordination across specialist teams.
